structural_rules: Each region is both habitat and civic pedagogy.
Kin are cultural participants in ecological relations, not biome mascots.
Beaverkin Way is connective infrastructure and corridor logic, not simply a sixth biome.

regions.name: Great Meadows Grassland
regions.gift: public belonging, visibility, abundance, shared feasts
regions.risk: overexposure and consensus flattening quieter needs
regions.repair_key: listening circles and shaded retreat zones

regions.name: Flowering Orchard
regions.gift: pollinator kinship, craft memory, sensory abundance
regions.risk: beauty becoming obligation, labor, or surveillance

regions.name: Nightforest Canopy
regions.gift: hidden routes, vigilance, nocturnal diplomacy, protective secrecy
regions.risk: watchfulness becoming ownership and secrecy becoming authority

regions.name: Mycelial Marshes
regions.gift: repair networks, filtration, decomposition wisdom, soft memory
regions.risk: absorption without consent and communal memory swallowing private grief

regions.name: Echoing Caverns
regions.gift: ancestry, resonance, mineral memory, underground shelter
regions.risk: old voices overruling the living and echoes impersonating truth
regions.repair_key: living-voice priority rites and thresholded disclosure

regions.name: Beaverkin Way
regions.gift: infrastructure as care through negotiated flow and safe-return routes

quarantined_noncanon: Beaverkin Way treated as merely another territorial biome
one-to-one kin species ownership of regions
connected terrain interpreted as automatically public or available
